channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-19
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `statcheck` and `escicheck` projects have made administrative updates and synchronized with other repositories, respectively, to ensure their tools remain up-to-date and effective. Meanwhile, the `oddpub` project has released a new version of its ontogenerator, version 7.3.0, and updated its documentation and Zenodo badge. The `docpluck`, `coarse`, and `regcheck` projects have also made significant updates, including bug fixes and enhancements to their web components and demo pages. These connections between projects demonstrate the collaborative spirit of our community, and we celebrate the progress being made to advance open-source research tools.

33 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

New releases: oddpub v7.3.0

Most active:

oddpub9 commits
regcheck9 commits
metacheck8 commits
tenzing7 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was worked on, where the commit added "japs jamovi" as per the message "Lakens: add japs jamovi". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. This specific addition was the sole change made in the repository since the last update. The commit message does not provide further details on the functionality or purpose of "japs jamovi", only that it was added by Lakens. There are no other changes or additions to report from the provided commit messages. The work was limited to a single branch and a single, specific addition.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 0612101 [data_check] add japs jamovi — Lakens
  • 1c49ec7 [dev] Merge pull request #333 from scienceverse/jw_plumber_bibr_api — Lisa DeBruine
  • f9da25f [dev] Merge pull request #332 from scienceverse/jw_bibr_read_passthrough — Lisa DeBruine
  • 0736a45 [dev] Merge pull request #331 from scienceverse/jw_grobid_text_fix — Lisa DeBruine
  • 9c2d675 [dev] Merge pull request #330 from scienceverse/jw_schema_sync_10_5 — Lisa DeBruine
  • 0a34c42 [data_check] process jasp files, better study categorization, better scale extraction. — Lakens
  • a4b8270 [data_check] additions — Lakens
  • 69afca1 [data_check] The OpenScales OSD export now exists end-to-end. Work spanned three files: — Lakens
  • c799dcf [data_check] improve cache, keep original data, better hardcoded folders — Lakens
  • 0c8303d [data_check] fix bugs, add archive report, gate large github repos, improve feedback — Lakens
  • 2615ab2 [data_check] updates osf download — Lakens
  • 58122ed [data_check] updates to functions, fix bugs — Lakens
  • ad4cb14 [data_check] improvements — Lakens
  • a73d52a [jw_llm_fixes] fix(llm): correct ollama-native gate (&& not %%); fail-fast param/model checks — Jakub Werner
  • d99956e [dev] feat(plumber): bibr-JSON REST API — validated uploads, HTML report, hardened runtime — Jakub Werner
  • 4daee8c [dev] feat(read): let bibr abstract and figure/table captions flow through — Jakub Werner
  • 00d98b8 [dev] fix(grobid): stop anchor normalization from corrupting paper text — Jakub Werner
  • 4d53313 [dev] fix(schema): sync bundled paper.json to bibr v10.5 — Jakub Werner
  • fdc5a1d [jw_fable_fixes] tests: fix test_that wrapper warnings, add expect_disjoint, env-aware .onLoad test — Jakub Werner
  • 994fb58 [jw_fable_fixes] modules: fix code_check green path, ref_miscitation report, traffic lights, stale imports — Jakub Werner

View all commits →

item.title: tenzing: 5 commits today
item.description:
marton-balazs-kovacs/tenzing

Work was done on the 'master' branch, where Alex O. Holcombe made changes to the wording, specifically "improving wording" and then further "rewording" the text. Additionally, Alex O. Holcombe "Italicize testimonials for emphasis" on the 'master' branch. The 'gh-pages' branch saw two updates, both by Anka, who "Built site for tenzing@0.4.1" with different commit hashes, e81218b and 99b3ee2. These 'gh-pages' updates were likely automated builds for the tenzing site at version 0.4.1. The changes on 'master' appear to be focused on text formatting and emphasis.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 72ba97c [gh-pages] Built site for tenzing@0.4.1: e81218b — Anka
  • e81218b [master] improving wording — Alex O. Holcombe
  • 2365b27 [master] reword — Alex O. Holcombe
  • 92af8bb [gh-pages] Built site for tenzing@0.4.1: 99b3ee2 — Anka
  • 99b3ee2 [master] Italicize testimonials for emphasis — Alex O. Holcombe
  • 0e4950f [gh-pages] Built site for tenzing@0.4.1: 388e7e3 — Anka
  • 388e7e3 [master] Update about.Rmd with recommendations, improve writing — Alex O. Holcombe

View all commits →
